text: The surgeon has completed the removal of adhesions from the anterior abdominal wall, showing a clear separation between the uterus and the intestines on the side wall.
The surgeon removes the uterus, detaching it from surrounding anatomical structures within the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon cuts the adhesions from all directions to completely liberate the affected anatomical structures.
The surgeon identifies film adhesions and cuts them from distant areas, revealing a mass close to the surgical field. The mass is further dissected from the back.
The surgeon continues the operation, working to completely liberate the adhesions from surrounding structures.
The surgeon cleans the blood vessels, ensuring that all vessels are completely closed, following the completion of adhesiolysis.
The surgeon places a plastic bag inside another plastic bag and wraps it securely, likely for specimen containment or isolation within the surgical field.
The surgeon prepares to remove the mass from the uterus after cutting film adhesions from all directions.
